title: The Bots And The Bees

jokes.text: Repeat: What I just said, immediately!
jokes.setup: Professor issues urgent crew summons
jokes.punchline: Redundantly repeats the exact same message as if clarifying
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth absurdist logic, perfectly economical structure

jokes.text: Sorry, Randy. Step away from the car and no one gets hurt! Ow! To the employment cave!
jokes.setup: Fry appears to be in some kind of confrontation
jokes.punchline: Abrupt transition to mundane work reference with physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Random scenario transition typical of Futurama cold opens, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, there, girl. Go on now! Get on back to Paraguay!
jokes.setup: Bender addressing something in cowboy vernacular
jokes.punchline: Specifically telling it to go to Paraguay
jokes.explanation: Absurdist specificity of Paraguay makes this memorable and quotable

jokes.text: We're being eaten by a giant spider! There's no time for that! The Professor needs us!
jokes.setup: Crew member reports being eaten by giant spider
jokes.punchline: Someone dismisses this life-threatening situation as less important than work meeting
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist priorities joke - being eaten is less urgent than a work meeting

jokes.text: What is it?! I'm on the edge of my butt!
jokes.setup: Fry expressing anticipation for announcement
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'edge of my seat' becomes anatomically literal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry malapropism - stupid but endearing, fits character perfectly

jokes.text: soda machine!
jokes.setup: Professor builds up 'greatest announcement in Planet Express history'
jokes.punchline: It's just a mundane soda machine
jokes.explanation: Classic anticlimax structure, perfectly economical delivery

jokes.text: You undersold it, Professor! Look, it has Slurm Loco! It's the extremiest!
jokes.setup: Fry responds to disappointing soda machine announcement
jokes.punchline: Genuinely excited about the mundane machine, plus made-up superlative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry enthusiasm for consumer products, 'extremiest' is a nice touch

jokes.text: Hey, what can I get y'all? Wow, you can talk? Shut up and give me a Slurm Loco.
jokes.setup: Bender amazed that vending machine can talk
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells talking machine to shut up and serve him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender rudeness - amazed by miracle then immediately dismissive

jokes.text: Y'all can't drink at work! This ain't a sawmill!
jokes.setup: Bender asks for alcoholic sodas at work
jokes.punchline: Bev's logic that sawmills are the only appropriate workplace for drinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific workplace hierarchy - sawmills as the boozing workplace standard

jokes.text: I call myself 'Bev.' And if you're looking for a smelly old can of booze, go look in the mirror.
jokes.setup: Bender insults the beverage machine
jokes.punchline: Bev calls Bender a smelly old can of booze
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback with robot-specific twist on mirror insult

jokes.text: And one more to wash that Slurm taste out of my mouth.
jokes.setup: Fry orders another Slurm
jokes.punchline: Claims he needs it to wash out the taste of the previous identical drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic - using identical drink as palate cleanser for itself

jokes.text: So I went to the bathroom and my pee was green. Pretty neat, huh? I was wondering who Shrekked in the toilet.
jokes.setup: Fry shares TMI about green urine from Slurm
jokes.punchline: Bender creates verb from Shrek character name
jokes.explanation: Creative pop culture reference transformation, though slightly cheap

jokes.text: Cut me some slack, preacher. I'm just a coal grinder's daughter trying to stay off the pole.
jokes.setup: Bev being criticized for enabling addiction
jokes.punchline: Reveals backstory as coal grinder's daughter avoiding stripping
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - working class background with stripper pole reference

jokes.text: With that big ol' caboose, it'd have to be a telephone pole!
jokes.setup: Bev mentions staying off 'the pole'
jokes.punchline: Bender implies she's too fat for a stripper pole, needs telephone pole
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender cruelty, competent structure but predictable fat joke

jokes.text: Well, I guess a fella's gotta talk big when he's sportin' a little shriveled-up antenna like that. What?! For your information, 'madam,' it's a grower, not a show-er!
jokes.setup: Bev insults Bender's antenna size
jokes.punchline: Bender defensively uses human penis excuse about robot antenna
jokes.explanation: Brilliant application of human insecurity to robot anatomy, perfectly in character

jokes.text: My antenna's fine. It's just fine. Ladies can't get enough Bender.
jokes.setup: Bender continuing antenna defense
jokes.punchline: Repeated reassurance showing deep insecurity despite bragging
jokes.explanation: Perfect insecure overcompensation, classic Bender psychology

jokes.text: Call me old-fashioned, but I like a little romance in an orgy.
jokes.setup: Bender commenting on lighting for robot hookup
jokes.punchline: Wanting romance in an orgy presented as traditional values
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of traditional romance values with orgy, perfectly economical

jokes.text: Just sitting here turning quarters into urine.
jokes.setup: Bender asks what Fry is doing glowing in the dark
jokes.punchline: Fry describes drinking as currency-to-waste conversion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reductive description of drinking, economical and quotable

jokes.text: We don't gotta put up with this. We got Poli Sci degrees.
jokes.setup: Robot prostitutes being mistreated
jokes.punchline: They have political science degrees as if this gives them options
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on useless degrees and sex work economics

jokes.text: Hot. I could bake a potato in my cleavage.
jokes.setup: Robot expressing how hot she is
jokes.punchline: Specific culinary application of cleavage heat
jokes.explanation: Amusing absurdist image but not deeply clever

jokes.text: Trashy? At least I don't put out for quarters.
jokes.setup: Bev calls robot prostitutes trashy
jokes.punchline: Prostitute points out vending machine literally services for quarters
jokes.explanation: Clever role reversal - prostitute calls out vending machine's transactional nature

jokes.text: All right, I've been waiting all day for an excuse to hit a lady.
jokes.setup: Fight brewing between Bev and prostitute
jokes.punchline: Bender excited about violence against women
jokes.explanation: In-character but relies on shock value over cleverness

jokes.text: Ooh, I'm so scared, No-Arms.
jokes.setup: Bev threatens to bash Bender's face
jokes.punchline: Bender points out she has no arms to fight with
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy observation, economical delivery

jokes.text: Uh, Fry? You're glowing like the Human Torch on prom night.
jokes.setup: Fry is glowing from radioactive soda
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture reference with prom night addition
jokes.explanation: Competent pop culture reference but not particularly inspired

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be standing on a rocky coast somewhere preventing shipwrecks?
jokes.setup: Fry glowing like a lighthouse
jokes.punchline: Reference to lighthouse's actual historical function
jokes.explanation: Clever lighthouse reference, shows Leela's intelligence

jokes.text: Nice, hate me because of the brightness of my skin.
jokes.setup: Fry being criticized for glowing
jokes.punchline: Frames it as racial discrimination parallel
jokes.explanation: Smart social commentary twist on discrimination

jokes.text: You okay, ma'am? I think your soda water broke.
jokes.setup: Bev appears to be leaking liquid
jokes.punchline: Pregnancy water breaking reference applied to soda machine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun connecting soda machine function to pregnancy, very economical

jokes.text: My God! Bev just had a baby in my cup!
jokes.setup: Revelation that robots can reproduce
jokes.punchline: Fry's concern is about his beverage container being used
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry priorities - miracle of robot birth vs. his cup hygiene

jokes.text: Aw, he's so cute. Wait. No, he isn't.
jokes.setup: Amy's initial positive reaction to baby
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal upon closer inspection
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective structure, very economical delivery

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. No way am I that kid's Dad!
jokes.setup: Baby looks like Bender
jokes.punchline: Immediate denial despite obvious resemblance
jokes.explanation: Classic deadbeat dad denial, perfectly in character for Bender

jokes.text: Wipe my tiny metal ass! Aw, crap.
jokes.setup: Bender denying paternity
jokes.punchline: Baby's first words are crude like Bender, immediate paternity proof
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paternity reveal through character-specific baby talk, perfectly timed

jokes.text: Aw, crap.
jokes.setup: Baby proves he's Bender's son with crude demand
jokes.punchline: Bender's understated reaction to confirmed parenthood
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction - economical expression of defeated acceptance

jokes.text: Ugh! In public?
jokes.setup: Bev breastfeeding robot baby
jokes.punchline: Amy's shock at public mechanical breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment applying human social norms to robot behavior

jokes.text: Are you telling me the stuff we did in private, and also twice on the sidewalk, made a baby robot? How?!
jokes.setup: Bender confused about robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about public sex while asking innocent question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender oversharing while maintaining innocent confusion

jokes.text: No. She was a religious fundamentalist. Plus she didn't have a mouth. It's an unusual combination.
jokes.setup: Asked if his mother explained robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Religious fundamentalist without mouth is unusual combination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd backstory - religious fundamentalism hampered by lack of mouth

jokes.text: We don't know either. Then prepare to be embarrassed.
jokes.setup: Crew admits they don't know about robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Professor threatens embarrassment through education
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for educational film parody segment

jokes.text: Sex Ed, Volume One, or... Pants Full of Shame!
jokes.setup: Educational film about robot reproduction
jokes.punchline: Shame-based title typical of outdated sex ed
jokes.explanation: Good parody of shame-based sex education film titles

jokes.text: Hey, he looks like me! His hideous appearance is a by-product of his hardware gearing up for an important mission: reproduction.
jokes.setup: Bender identifies with Gerald in educational film
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately calls appearance hideous
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing of insult immediately after Bender's identification

jokes.text: The best he can do is Francine.
jokes.setup: Gerald must find female robot
jokes.punchline: Narrator's brutal assessment that Francine is Gerald's best option
jokes.explanation: Brutally economical putdown in educational film format

jokes.text: It's all perfectly natural. Let's watch, aroused, as Gerald's antenna uploads a binary file to Francine's internal drive.
jokes.setup: Educational film describing robot sex
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate suggestion that audience should be aroused
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of educational film propriety with creepy narrator

jokes.text: Except masturbation. That's just wrong.
jokes.setup: Everything your body does is perfectly natural
jokes.punchline: Sudden moral judgment about masturbation
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of religious hypocrisy in sex education, excellent timing

jokes.text: Bev will get custody, and you'll be a deadbeat Dad who never even bothers to know his own son. You really think so? I know so. You're absolutely horrible in every way.
jokes.setup: Leela predicting Bender will be deadbeat dad
jokes.punchline: Bender finds this prediction comforting, Leela confirms his awfulness
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamics - Bender comforted by confirmation of his terribleness

jokes.text: You're sweet, Leela.
jokes.setup: Leela tells Bender he's horrible in every way
jokes.punchline: Bender takes this as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - taking insults as affection, very economical

jokes.text: So here's my certificate of abandonment drawn up by our notary! That'll be ten bucks.
jokes.setup: Bender abandoning his child
jokes.punchline: Has formal certificate plus notary charging fee mid-abandonment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist bureaucracy of child abandonment with fee collection

jokes.text: Damn baby's drivin' me crazy! He won't stop giggling no matter how much I scream at him!
jokes.setup: Bev complaining about baby's behavior
jokes.punchline: Baby's happiness despite being screamed at
jokes.explanation: Good irony - baby's resilient happiness despite abuse

jokes.text: I still got womanly needs! Who's gonna look twice at me with this dirty leech hangin' off my dairy nozzle?
jokes.setup: Bev explaining why she can't keep the baby
jokes.punchline: Crude description of baby as impediment to sex life
jokes.explanation: Crudely effective character moment but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Bend some other stuff! Ben'! Ben'!
jokes.setup: Baby Ben loves watching Bender bend things
jokes.punchline: Baby's first word approximating 'bend' sounds like his own name
jokes.explanation: Clever name origin story - Ben named after his desire to 'bend'

jokes.text: I'm gonna call him Ben... after the first half of me... Bender.
jokes.setup: Bender deciding on a name for his son
jokes.punchline: Explains Ben comes from first half of Bender
jokes.explanation: Sweet parental moment with Bender's characteristic self-centeredness in naming

jokes.text: You bended it like a 'petzel.'
jokes.setup: Ben praising Bender's bending
jokes.punchline: Child's mispronunciation of 'pretzel'
jokes.explanation: Cute child speech pattern, establishes character relationship

jokes.text: Leela was wrong! Leela was wrong!
jokes.setup: Leela apologizing for doubting Bender's parenting
jokes.punchline: Bender teaching son to chant insults about Leela
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty Bender moment - teaching child to insult crew member

jokes.text: But my mommy had no arms! Does that mean I can never be a bender?
jokes.setup: Bender explains arm control software inheritance
jokes.punchline: Ben realizes his mother's lack of arms dooms his bending dreams
jokes.explanation: Good logical consequence of earlier character detail

jokes.text: I hate to crush a boy's dreams, but what the heck.
jokes.setup: Doctor about to deliver bad news to child
jokes.punchline: Doctor admits he enjoys crushing dreams
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny medical professional cruelty, good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Don't tell my son what he can and can't do! You may know what's in his head, but you don't know what's in his heart! There's no slot in there either. I said shut up!
jokes.setup: Bender's passionate defense of his son's potential
jokes.punchline: Doctor literal-mindedly points out heart also lacks expansion slot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of inspirational speech with technical literalism

jokes.text: I'd like to thank the Preacherbot for the introduction and also for not circumcising me again, am I right? Twice was enough.
jokes.setup: Ben giving coming-of-age speech
jokes.punchline: References being circumcised twice by robot preacher
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd robot religious ritual reference

jokes.text: I could take him or leave him.
jokes.setup: Someone noting Bender must be proud of his son
jokes.punchline: Bender's indifferent response despite clear emotional attachment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender emotional denial, very economical delivery

jokes.text: I've come to take him and leave with him! She beat you to both things.
jokes.setup: Bev declaring her intentions redundantly
jokes.punchline: Fry points out she said the same thing twice
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation of redundant statement

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, I'm still less unfitter than you. Says who?! Says this document you gave me when you tried to abandon Ben first!
jokes.setup: Custody battle over who's more unfit parent
jokes.punchline: Bev uses Bender's own abandonment certificate against him
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier plot point, poetic justice

jokes.text: That doesn't prove I'm an unfit parent! I was drunk and violent when I signed that!
jokes.setup: Bender defending against unfit parent accusation
jokes.punchline: His defense actually makes him sound worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-incriminating defense, very Bender logic

jokes.text: That's it, Fry! For a guy who's not too bright, you're too damn bright! Now, get out! And don't come back until someone finds a use for a million-watt idiot!
jokes.setup: Fry's radioactive glow is problematic
jokes.punchline: Professor's paradoxical insult about brightness levels
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on intellectual vs. literal brightness

jokes.text: That's it, Fry! For a guy who's not too bright, you're too damn bright! Now, get out! And don't come back until someone finds a use for a million-watt idiot!
jokes.setup: Fry's glowing is disturbing Bender's grief
jokes.punchline: Calls Fry not bright but too bright, wants use for million-watt idiot
jokes.explanation: Good contradictory insult - not bright vs too bright, million-watt idiot is memorable

jokes.text: Mom, will I ever get to see Dad again? No, he died yesterday. Rust monsters ate his face. Sweet dreams!
jokes.setup: Child asking innocent question about father
jokes.punchline: Mother's cruel lie about father's gruesome death
jokes.explanation: Brutally dark parenting moment with robot-specific death method

jokes.text: Only a fool plans more than a few centuries ahead, son.
jokes.setup: Ben asking about future plans
jokes.punchline: Bender's robot-appropriate time scale for short-term planning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant robot perspective on time - centuries as short-term planning

jokes.text: You're under arrest for kidnapping and burning raccoons without a permit!
jokes.setup: Police arresting Bender and Ben
jokes.punchline: Raccoon burning apparently requires permit, not prohibited
jokes.explanation: Absurdist bureaucracy - animal cruelty is fine with proper permits

jokes.text: There's a dam! Damn! There's a grate! Great!
jokes.setup: Chase scene with obstacles
jokes.punchline: Each obstacle announcement sounds like reaction to previous one
jokes.explanation: Clever homophone wordplay in action sequence

jokes.text: Yeah, this ain't gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Ben attempting to bend the grate to save them
jokes.punchline: Immediate realistic assessment of his inability
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of heroic moment, very economical

jokes.text: Is that even necessary?
jokes.setup: Police brutality occurring off-screen
jokes.punchline: Child's matter-of-fact question about excessive force
jokes.explanation: Good innocent observation about police violence

jokes.text: I'm amazed someone who dispenses such warm soda, could be so coldhearted!
jokes.setup: Leela criticizing Bev's parenting
jokes.punchline: Temperature-based metaphor connecting soda service to personality
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay connecting character function to personality trait

jokes.text: I'm gonna buy a drink just so I can throw it in your face!
jokes.setup: Fry angry at Bev
jokes.punchline: Will support her business to express his anger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant consumer logic failure. Perfect Fry moment of good intentions undermining themselves.

jokes.text: You got knocked up by a police officer while he was looking for your kidnapped child? Sure did! I still got it.
jokes.setup: Leela incredulous about timing of Bev's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Bev proud of her sexual opportunism during crisis
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny character moment - pride in inappropriate timing

jokes.text: Damn it, I love that little guy so much! I'd sacrifice any four of you if it would help him bend even a little!
jokes.setup: Bender expressing love for his son
jokes.punchline: Willing to sacrifice specific number of friends for son's benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - genuine love expressed through casual cruelty to friends

jokes.text: He'd be able to bend, but he'd lose all his memories. He wouldn't even remember who you are.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining bending card installation consequences
jokes.punchline: The cure eliminates the relationship that motivated it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tragic irony - achieving the goal destroys its meaning

jokes.text: No! I said with the mouth you're looking at.
jokes.setup: Ben asks if Bender is crying
jokes.punchline: Bender's weird denial about which mouth is speaking
jokes.explanation: Bizarre Bender deflection, suggests he has multiple mouths

jokes.text: Who are you again?
jokes.setup: Bender apologizing for failing Ben
jokes.punchline: Ben no longer remembers Bender due to memory wipe
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking callback to earlier warning, very economical delivery

jokes.text: Who are you again?
jokes.setup: Ben after losing his memories
jokes.punchline: Doesn't remember his father who sacrificed everything for him
jokes.explanation: Devastating emotional payoff - ultimate parental sacrifice rendered meaningless with perfect economy

tmdb_overview: Bender fathers a child with the office soda machine.
